accurate.why: The disapproval/nullification matches the bill text, and the contextual claims (DOE, December 26, 2024 publication, amended standards, technologically feasible and economically justified) are verified as accurate.

no_hallucinations.why: All added context (DOE as administering agency, rule's date and content) is verifiable and accurate, not fabricated.

states_purpose.why: The summary clearly states the resolution nullifies the named DOE rule.

changes_to_existing_law.why: The summary correctly explains the resolution renders the DOE rule of no force or effect, accurately describing the legal change.

exceptions_conditions.why: The one-section disapproval resolution contains no material exceptions or carve-outs to summarize.

effective_dates.why: The resolution specifies no effective dates or deadlines beyond enactment, so none need be noted.

major_provisions_covered.why: The summary captures the sole operative effect: disapproval of the rule so it has no force or effect.

objective_tone.why: The summary is neutral and descriptive with no advocacy or editorializing.

coherent.why: The summary is clear, well-organized, and internally consistent.

concise.why: The summary is brief and free of padding or repetition.

correct_entities.why: The Department of Energy and the named rule are identified correctly.

correct_figures.why: The only figure, the December 26, 2024 submission date, matches the bill text and Federal Register record.
